reviews.title: Pretty much a Fenix 6S with a better battery
reviews.text:
I figured I'd give my 6S to the wife and try the 7S Sapphire Solar. In general the watches are nearly identical, but the 7S does have a noticably better battery. When we go hiking, we get identical information, the 7S doesn't need L5 GPS but it works fine. I like the addition of the touch display. Lastly, Garmin is finally getting their act together on software, the 6S and 7S both work well with Garmin Golf and Connect, lots of good data and charts.

There are a few more data pages on the watch for me to look at than on the 6S. I suspect the 6S could have them too but Garmin wants you to upgrade.

Bottom line, if you have a 6, wait for the 8, maybe it will have ECG?

reviews.title: worth the try, gorgeous clear display, ease of use, fits my 7" wrist,
reviews.text:
It's pretty good, especially if you dont need a name brand. It has a beautifully clear display. Easy to install app and pairs well with no hassle. Has all the bells and whistles of name brand products. While I prefer fitbit due to familiarity.

I havent put it entirely through its paces. I noticed that it was registering nearly same heart rate as my fitbit sense. I havent it worn it long enough to determine blood oxygen. There is still a lot to learn. The learning curve feels a little clunky compared to my fitbit.

Has ability to connect to Alexa, take and make calls, music. Can make changes through app or scroll through the screen face.

The watch band fits my 7" wrist and feels comfortable enough. Typical silicone band. It is attached via pins, so I imagine it is possible to swap it out but not sure. I may try some old bands to see if it is doable. I'd also prefer for it to have a soft protective cover (bumper guard) to protect screen and edges.

I still prefer the ease of using a name brand, but this has a good looking interface. Main thing is to see how accurate it is. I think it has been fine for heart rate, steps, but will need more sleep time for seeing how well it tracks sleep and oxygen.

I dont rate on price, but I comment. This is pretty reasonable, but it looks like there are many similar products for less. So compared to a name brand of similar features this is a bargain. Compared to other no name generics, this is competitive. Some are easily $20 less. I noticed the price on this vacillates. I think I got it for $70, and have seen it as low as 35-40.

reviews.title: Pretty good
reviews.text:
Pretty good smartwatch. I needed something to replace my Galaxy Active, but didn't want to spend $100+ on something with a horrible battery life. I bought this one and for a while I was disappointed. I had to charge the watch each night and the steps weren't as accurate. I changed the setting so that the screen doesn't turn on when you move your wrist and that has saved the battery big time. I'm on day 4 and only down to 50% which means I'm now able to track sleep as well. I still think the steps aren't as accurate because it almost matches the steps in my phone's health app, but I'm notorious for leaving my phone places. I also miss being able to limit the options for exercise types in the watch face. I have to scroll through a long list each time. Overall, pretty good product for a cheaper price.

Update: I originally gave this 5 stars, but I have to take 2 away. A few days ago, the watch all of a sudden stopped counting steps. For the price, it was perfect until it stopped working. Back to the drawing board.

reviews.title: Best gift ever
reviews.text: Bought this for a 9 year old. It’s amazing. It has so many things on it and is easy to use. She shows it to everyone who will look at it and she feels like it’s just like her big brothers and sisters watches. Very happy.

reviews.title: 2G is Dead
reviews.text: This watch says that it utilizes a sim card with 2g service. This service is nearly dead everywhere. The sim card area in the watch is a little tricky to figure out. You have to open the back and then lift up the battery slightly. The sim card slot is located under the battery. You have to slide(left/right) and lift up the metal sim card cover like a door. Insert the sim card and lay the metal cover down over it and slide it into place to lock it. This watch is not waterproof. The watch works as specified except for the 2g calling. 2g service is not available in my area, so make sure you check if your area still has it. You have to buy your own sim card and they recommend Speedtalk service. Check their coverage map for 2g service.

APP PAIRING AND BATTERY: pairing is simple, by using Bluetooth 4.2, automatically sync your device to a phone using iOS 8. 0/Android OS 4. 4 or above. reviews.title: Great watch for a great price
reviews.text: I was skeptical about the watch as I had purchased other watches only to have them fail miserably. This watch turned out great. The most important thing to do is read the manual. Especially about how to wear the watch, which is a little higher than a normal watch. This will make the accuracy much better. The step counter is controlled by arm movement not actual steps, so if you walk stiff armed , it will be inaccurate. Move your arms when walking , the watch will be much more accurate.Make sure you go into the app on your phone and activate the exercises your interested in. This will help the watch to track it better. Also activate the media part of the app for sms,email etc. You can also monitor who is calling you, so you can choose to answer you phone or not. I am extremely pleased with my purchase and will be watching for other products from this company.

The set up was fairly easy - I received the watch in the early evening and had it set up within half an hour ( I am in my seventies). This is my 3rd watch - first was very expensive and too invasive - second watch cost twice as much as this one and quit charging in the second week - third times a charm.

title: SAMSUNG GEAR S3 FRONTIER Smartwatch 46MM (Bluetooth Only) - Dark Grey (Renewed)
description:
Stay in touch, without reaching for your phone, with the smartwatch that’s engineered for adventure. Convenience being the key feature, the watch offers fitness tracking, app access and watch face customization. With the Gear S3, you can pay right from your wrist and track your location as well. The Gear S3 is compatible with both Android and iOS smartphones.
Never Miss a Moment
The Gear S3 is Bluetooth connected so you can receive texts, emails, and alerts without reaching for your phone. With a built-in speaker and microphone, you can also make and answer calls hands-free. Just rotate the distinctive steel bezel to easily access apps and notifications so you can instantly check texts, updates, and more.
Pay with Your Watch
With Samsung Pay on your Gear S3, you can easily make payments with your watch almost anywhere you can swipe or tap a credit card - even without your phone or wallet.
Get Out There
Whether you’re off-roading or exploring a new city, the built-in GPS tracks your activity and makes it easy to share your location.
Designed for The Elements
The military-grade Gear S3 is tough enough to handle the elements. It resists water, dust, extreme temperatures, and the occasional drop so you can venture out with confidence.
Go for Days
Go nonstop for days on a single charge.4 When it’s time to recharge, the wireless charger lets you power up without slowing down.
Tailored to You
Make the Gear S3 your own. Customize your watch face, choosing from thousands of striking colors and designs. Plus easily swap out the standard 22mm band.
features: The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
The Samsung Gear S3 has an always-on watch face and distinctive steel bezel that you can rotate to access apps and notifications.
Text, call and get notifications directly from your watch through your Bluetooth connected smartwatch.
Access the apps you use every day on your Gear S3. Catch a ride, check scores, stream music, and more.
With the built-in S Health app, track your steps, monitor your heart rate, and more.

reviews.title: Just over 90 days and it completely died
reviews.text: Rrally liked this watch at first. The battery did not last very long, even after adjusting settings. Thought it was the battery so we bought a new one, nope just completely died now. The new battery is fine, charges up and works, but the watch will not even turn on with new battery in it.

reviews.text: Love this watch

reviews.title: Husband LOVES This
reviews.text: I bought this for my husband for his birthday and Father's Day and he loves it. He used to have a Galaxy S9, but dropped it into a lake while we were fishing *face palm*. But he ended up getting an old S7 from a family member and the watch connects with that as well. The only complaint I have heard from him is about the band, which was easy and cheap to replace with a metal material that didn't stick to his wrist as much as the rubbery band that comes with the watch.

reviews.title: Good watch
reviews.text:
I bought this when it first came out, I needed it's walking and stair climbing metrics.

It's big, heavy, and if you're at all active, it's on the charge cradle for around 1 hour. The big, heavy part, some like it, I wish it were a little smaller. The battery life, it's an early version, I didn't expect much, my next watch, 20 day minimum.

All in all, it's a very functional watch, feature laden, and good, as long as Samsung supports the OS

title: Kimnix Smart Watch 2022(Make Call Receive/Dial), 1.72 in HD Full Touch Screen Fitness Tracker with Bluetooth Call/Text/Heart Rate/SpO2/Sleep Monitor Smartwatch for Android iOS Mens Women (Black)

reviews.title: Fair features for the price
reviews.text:
First of all, you must keep in consideration that this is a $50 watch. That being said I will state what I consider are the pros and cons:

Pros
Price, battery life (more than the typical 1-day of apple watches), capable of answering and dialing within the watch (Bluetooth required) and blood oxygen and heart rate sensors.

Cons
Strap does not hold to watch and you must keep correcting it (solved with another strap?), blood pressure monitor? How???, you only have five watch faces at any given time but you can only replace one of the five - the others are permanently set, you get to edit the fourth one by adding a photo

Overall, there are cheaper and better watches like the WYZE (which I own and has better quality) but lack the phone answering and dialing features.

I will be replacing the strap with another type to see if it holds.

reviews.title: It does not keep all of the apps current. That is what I bought it for.

reviews.title: Some Good Aspects, But Several Annoying Issues
reviews.text:
This CNPGD Smart Watch Fitness Tracker had the potential to be really great. In terms of appearance, the body of the watch combined with the band are sleek and attractive. The watch is available in 4 different color schemes (Black, Pink, Red, and Blue/Silver) and each looks aesthetically pleasing. I received the Black one and really liked the clean lines and modern design. The band fits around the wrist comfortably as well.

Before trying to turn the watch on, I decided to charge it fully overnight. I followed the instructions and photos on the instruction sheet and tried to remove the band from the watch's charging port end. Unfortunately, it was so difficult to remove that I thought I was going to damage the watch or the band at some point as I worked up a sweat trying to pry/pull it apart. I finally managed to insert the lip of a plastic spoon into a sliver of space I had gotten between the band and the watch. I kept working at it until I was finally able to get the band off. I really do not look forward to repeating this process.

Once the watch was charged, I downloaded the WearFit 2.0 App and after a couple of tries, was able to pair my phone with the watch. There are some annoying aspects about the App. In one area, I was able to specify customary units such as pounds and feet versus the default kilograms and centimeters, but in another area, I was unable to make the change. When using the watch, as I started to toggle through the functions, I realized that there was no way to toggle backwards. So if I swiped past my desired function such as the stopwatch feature, I needed to swipe past all the other functions again until I got back to the stopwatch feature. Swiping right or left or pressing the Home button does the same thing and only moves the choice of functions in one direction, forward.

I went out for a long walk and compared the watch's step and distance counter against my phone's fitness app with GPS and a very accurate pocket pedometer. After the walk, my phone fitness app, pedometer, and this watch all gave roughly the same measurement for the mileage. Unfortunately, the step count of the watch was about 2,000 steps off from the other two devices. it's unfortunate that this watch has so many annoying issues as the features such as the BP, O2, and other health trackers are quite handy. I would give this fitness tracker watch more stars if they just made some upgrades/updates to the app and maybe the watch's firmware.

features: 【Fitness Tracker & Blood Oxygen Monitor】 GRV N29 smartwatch tracks your daily steps, heart rate, calorie, sleep quality and distance throughout the day, and records your activity data in the app. Contains 10 different sports modes: indoor walking, outdoor walking, indoor running, outdoor running, outdoor cycling, yoga, spinning, boating, etc. You can measure the body's blood oxygen saturation after exercise or in normal times.

【HD Color Touch Screen & Customized Watch Face】 N29 adopts 1.4" HD sensitive touch screen. The screen brightness is adjustable, which enable you to find a suitable brightness for different using environment, such as at night or in the sun. You can customize the watch face by choosing one from the watch or from the app. There are 30+ dials (Continually updated) in the APP and you can also upload any photos of your family, pets, etc.

【Receive Notifications & Compatibility】 Receive notifications of incoming calls, text messages, messages (such as Facebook, Twitter, Youtube, Instagram, etc.) from smartphone. Note: The watch cannot answer/reply/send texts, messages or calls. The free "InxFit" App supports smartphones with iOS 9.0/Android 5.0 and higher and Bluetooth 5.0.

【Long Battery Life & IP68 Waterproof】 A full charge takes 2 to 3 hours and it can last about 7 days. The watch has a waterproof rating of IP68, which makes it shower proof and wearable in a swimming pool. It would be safe even to submerge up to a depth of 1.5 metres for around half an hour. The strap is changeable and made of soft silicone, very comfortable.

【More Functions & Quality Services】There are other convenient functions, such as music control/drink water reminder/timer/weather reminder/blood oxygen monitor/stopwatch/alarm/breathing training. The functions are easy to access and use. The app syncs well and quickly with the watch. If you encounter any quality or use problems, please feel free to contact us through order and we will give you a satisfactory answer.

reviews.title: My teenager stole it from me it’s that good
reviews.text: This may lack features of other watches in its price range but it’s way better.  It’s simple and sleek, and it gets the Job Done.  The notifications, and Text are easy to read, and it’s slim but still carries a charge.  The gold tone hasn’t faded and the band holds up to the ravages of a middle schooler.

reviews.text: Have an android phone and this works wonderful.  Great monitoring of heart rate very accurate.

reviews.title: Just ok
reviews.text: The band fits as expected. I like the magnetic design and the magnet holds well. My problem with it is it gets caught on fabric and has pulled on some of my clothes. It also pinches sometimes.

title: Beantech Gold Engage Plus Smartwatch for iOS and Android with 8GB of Music Storage and Leather Strap
description: Beantech Engage Plus smart Watch provides full function applications in a classically elegant design. Stay healthy by using a full array of fitness apps, then back up your data on Beantech Watch app. Send and receive phone calls, texts and emails on the Watch. Load and store over 1000 songs in the music Vault. Beantech Engage Plus works with both iPhone and Android phones. Bit Watch app available on Apple app store and Google play store at No additional cost. Polished Silver with Brown Calfskin Leather strap.
features: FUNCTION - full function smart watch in a Classic design. Ips touch screen for bright clear viewing and interfacing with pre-loaded apps. Works with iPhone or Android.
COMMUNICATIONS - send and receive phone calls, texts and email. Receives notifications from phone apps.
FITNESS- count daily steps, check your heart rate, monitor your sleep patterns and upload to Bit watch app to keep track. Compare to daily goals.
MUSIC VAULT - 8G of on board storage allows you to load and store over 1000 songs on the watch, then take them with you without your phone!
BIT WATCH APP - interfaces with Bit watch app to allow communications, sync time and store fitness data. Bit watch app is available in Apple app store and Google play store at no additional cost.
COMPATIBILITY - iPhone - iOS 7.1 or higher and model 5S or newer. Android phones - Android 4.4 or higher.
